text: A 10-week program of cycling and running 40 min/day, 6 days/week increases VO2max by 11-20%.
Reducing training intensity by one-third while maintaining frequency and duration causes VO2max to decline below fully trained levels but remain above pre-training levels.
Reducing training intensity by two-thirds while maintaining frequency and duration causes greater VO2max loss than a one-third reduction.
Short-term endurance (~5 min effort) can be maintained with a one-third reduction in training intensity when frequency and duration are preserved.
Long-term endurance declines by 21% with a one-third reduction in training intensity and by 30% with a two-thirds reduction, even when frequency and duration are maintained.
Training-induced left ventricular mass gains (~15% increase) are completely lost when training intensity is reduced, regardless of whether the reduction is one-third or two-thirds.
Training intensity is the critical variable for maintaining aerobic fitness gains; maintaining only frequency and duration is insufficient.
